behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: This failure is about handoff across modules (file load + env overlay + validation + error rendering). No real OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency is required by the behavior text, so System/Agentic are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real config loader with a temp config file missing `database.host` and no matching `TOOL_` env var; assert the raised startup error includes the exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: The risk is module interaction between env resolution and path-building in the loader. The behavior text does not require subprocess/HTTP/external contract checks or AI output, so System/Agentic are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory, place a config there, invoke the loader, and assert it reads from `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tool/config.yaml` instead of `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: This is precedence logic across two sources (parsed YAML + env mapping), i.e., cross-module orchestration. No explicit real external contract check or AI/model dependency is in the behavior text, so higher levels are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a config file value and a conflicting `TOOL_...` env var for the same key, run the real loader, and assert the resulting config uses the env value.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: This depends on integration with the actual YAML parser and error-mapping logic, not just one pure function. System is rejected because behavior text only requires parser-derived line info, not subprocess/real external API/explicit external contract round-trip; Agentic/Workflow are inapplicable.
behaviors.test_description: Create malformed YAML in a temp config file, run the loader, and assert the surfaced error message includes the parser-reported line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly requires proving an externally visible side effect (directory/file creation) via round-trip observation, which triggers the SYSTEM TRIAD (side-effect visibility). Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI steps are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Start from a clean temp home/config root with no config directory, run the CLI startup path, then verify on disk that the directory and defaults file were created and readable before normal startup continues.
